mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and rising consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the mechanism fails to produce a positive information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales exceeding 1.0% signals immediate consumer demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where retail sales drop by more than 1.0%, or the trigger condition shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts market repricing of risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth correlates with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy falls below 48%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts during a low-rate environment signals expanding household balance sheets and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.
